creator: samflanker@gmail.com
text:
uncorrect jail stop

Fix: problem in uncorrect work '-i' option of '/usr/sbin/jail' program

have 2 solutions:

1. edit /etc/rc.d/jail file

-----------------------------------------------------



-----------------------------------------------------

2. hack '/usr/sbin/jail' program--52z1CUwLzUK5CXflP5JMcOVKFaRoJxqSq2Yz3oCOlYEMsvk7
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.diff"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.diff"

diff -ruN /usr/src/etc/rc.d /etc/rc.d/jail
--- /usr/src/etc/rc.d/jail	2009-10-25 02:10:29.000000000 +0100
+++ /etc/rc.d/jail	2009-12-09 11:02:48.000000000 +0100
@@ -635,7 +635,7 @@
 			i=$((i + 1))
 		done
 
-		eval ${_setfib} jail ${_flags} -i ${_rootdir} ${_hostname} \
+		eval ${_setfib} jail -i ${_flags} ${_rootdir} ${_hostname} \
 			\"${_addrl}\" ${_exec_start} > ${_tmp_jail} 2>&1
 
 		if [ "$?" -eq 0 ] ; then
How-To-Repeat: repeat commands (after jail created):

/etc/rc.d/jail start && /etc/rc.d/jail stop
..
/usr/sbin/jls

and see list of dont stoped jails
